issue.title: Enhancement: 'Jump to terminal' button on issue detail when work is active (E10)

phases.title: terminalStore: project-scoped workId→terminalId association map + pure resolveWorkTerminal helper + test
verification: terminalStore.ts has `terminalByWorkIdByProject: Record<string, Record<string, string>>` in TerminalState, initialised to {} in initialState.
`associateWorkTerminal(issueNumber, terminalId)` action sets the project-scoped entry immutably and is exposed on the store.
`cleanupProject` removes the project's entry from terminalByWorkIdByProject.
`terminalByWorkIdByProject` is NOT included in the partialize return (session-only).
`resolveWorkTerminal` is exported, pure, and returns null for missing association / dead terminal, `{tabId, terminalId}` for a live one (including non-root split leaves).
New terminalStore.test.ts covers the three resolveWorkTerminal cases and passes under `pnpm test`.
phases.summary: Added project-scoped terminalByWorkIdByProject map + associateWorkTerminal action to terminalStore; cleanupProject prunes it; not persisted (partialize excludes it) since terminalIds regenerate on reload. New pure exported resolveWorkTerminal(workMap,tabs,issueNumber) walks split trees via getTerminalIds and returns {tabId,terminalId} or null. New terminalStore.test.ts covers 4 cases incl. non-root split leaves.

phases.title: Record associations at the 3 issue-scoped dispatch sites + add the Jump-to-terminal button to IssueDetail
phases.content:
Wire the Phase-1 store up to the dispatch sites and the detail panel.

### Dispatch site 1 — KanbanBoard.tsx
`triggerExecution(issueNumber, fromColumn)` already resolves the active terminal via `executeInTerminal` → `getActiveTerminalId()`. Restructure so it captures the terminalId: call `getActiveTerminalId()` directly, and if non-null call `useTerminalStore.getState().associateWorkTerminal(issueNumber, terminalId)` before `executeInTerminal`. Do the same in `confirmShip` using `shipConfirmation.issueNumber`.

### Dispatch site 2 — IssuesSection.tsx
`handleRunYolo(issue)` resolves `activeTab.activeTerminalId`. Add `useTerminalStore.getState().associateWorkTerminal(issue.number, activeTab.activeTerminalId)` right before the `write_terminal` invoke.

### Dispatch site 3 — useCommandActions.ts
The contextual `Run X on #N (current issue)` variant is issue-scoped. Change `writeToActiveTerminal` to return the terminalId it wrote to (`Promise<string | null>`); in the contextual `execute`, await it and call `associateWorkTerminal(selectedIssue, terminalId)` when non-null. The bare (non-contextual) command ignores the return value.

### IssueDetail.tsx — the button
Import `useTerminalStore`, `terminalFocusRegistry`, `resolveWorkTerminal` from the terminal store, and `useLayoutStore`. Subscribe to the project-scoped `terminalByWorkIdByProject[activeProjectId]` map and `tabsByProject[activeProjectId]`. Compute `const jumpTarget = work ? resolveWorkTerminal(workMap, tabs, issue.number) : null;`.

When `jumpTarget` is non-null, render a `detail-action-btn` in the existing `<div className="detail-actions">` block (next to "Open in GitHub" / "Edit Plan"), labelled "Jump to terminal" with a small terminal-glyph SVG. onClick:
```ts
useLayoutStore.getState().setActiveView('terminal');
useTerminalStore.getState().setActiveTab(jumpTarget.tabId);
terminalFocusRegistry.focus(jumpTarget.terminalId);
```
The button is simply not rendered when `jumpTarget` is null (no active work, or no live associated terminal) — satisfies "gracefully hide when no association exists."

### Scope boundaries
- No new dependencies. No Rust changes.
- The association is best-effort and session-only — if the user never dispatched an issue-scoped command for this issue from the desktop UI, the button simply doesn't appear (running `/tiki:yolo` by hand in a terminal is not tracked; that's an accepted limitation, the same one E10 implies by saying "when an issue has active work *with a known terminal*").
verification: KanbanBoard.tsx `triggerExecution` and `confirmShip` call `associateWorkTerminal` with the issue number and the active terminalId before dispatching.
useCommandActions.ts: `writeToActiveTerminal` returns the terminalId; the contextual `Run X on #N` variant associates it with `selectedIssue`.
IssueDetail.tsx renders a 'Jump to terminal' button in `.detail-actions` only when `work` is present AND `resolveWorkTerminal` returns a live target.
Clicking the button sets the view to 'terminal', sets the active tab to the resolved tabId, and focuses the resolved terminalId.
The button is absent when there is no active work or no live associated terminal.
`pnpm build` passes and `pnpm test` is green.

Manual smoke (user): drag an issue to Execute on the kanban (or run a contextual command on a selected issue), open that issue's detail panel, click 'Jump to terminal' — the app switches to the Terminal view, the correct tab, and the pane is focused.
phases.summary: Wired associateWorkTerminal into all 3 issue-scoped dispatch sites: KanbanBoard triggerExecution + confirmShip, IssuesSection handleRunYolo, useCommandActions contextual Run-on-#N variant (writeToActiveTerminal now returns the terminalId). IssueDetail renders a "Jump to terminal" button in .detail-actions when work is active and resolveWorkTerminal finds a live target; click switches to terminal view, sets the active tab, and focuses the pane. Button hidden when no live association.
